core_mechanism: a recursively self-similar bigraded combinatorial Hopf algebra on rooted trees, in which a grafting one-cocycle operator builds larger structures by nesting whole structures inside a new node, and iterating the coproduct enumerates the dimensions of each graded subspace as partition-type generating functions
brief_reason: The abstract builds a Hopf algebra of rooted trees bigraded by node count n and degree of primitivity k, generated recursively by the Hochschild one-cocycle B_+ that 'plugs one set of divergences into another,' and computes graded-subspace dimensions by iterating the coproduct, yielding counts 'familiar from the theory of partitions.'
